note: Block Region Permissions dynamically generates one permission per theme region (administer {theme} {region}) and filters the Block layout form, block add/edit form, block_access, and the edit/delete routes so a role can only manage blocks in permitted regions. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On Drupal 11, core's 'Administer blocks' permission lets a role manage blocks in every region. I want a role that can only manage blocks in specific theme regions. Which contrib module does this and how does it expose the control?
evals.expected_output: Use the Block Region Permissions module. It dynamically generates one permission per enabled theme's region so you can grant block administration on a per-theme, per-region basis instead of the all-or-nothing 'Administer blocks' permission.

evals.prompt: In Block Region Permissions, what is the machine-name pattern and title of the permission generated for a theme region, for example the Content region of the Claro theme?
evals.expected_output: The permission machine name is 'administer {theme_key} {region_key}' (e.g. 'administer claro content'), titled 'Administer: {Theme name} - {Region name}' (e.g. 'Administer: Claro - Content'). They are generated by Permissions::get() from each enabled, non-hidden theme's regions.

evals.prompt: If I grant a role only the per-region permissions from Block Region Permissions, can they use the Block layout page? Is any core permission still needed, and what is the caveat?
evals.expected_output: The core 'administer blocks' permission is still required to reach the Block layout UI at all; the module only filters within that page. Caveat: 'Administer blocks' still exposes block pages this module does not manage (disable/enable, place-block library, add), so pair it with Block Content Permissions to restrict the rest.

evals.prompt: How does Block Region Permissions stop a user from editing or deleting a block in a region they lack permission for, even if they hit the edit URL directly? Name the mechanisms.
evals.expected_output: It implements hook_block_access() forbidding the 'update' and 'delete' operations for blocks whose region the user cannot administer, and a RouteSubscriber adds a _custom_access requirement (AccessControlHandler::blockFormAccess) to the entity.block.edit_form and entity.block.delete_form routes, so direct URLs are access-checked, not just hidden.

evals.prompt: On the Block layout page and the block configure form, what does Block Region Permissions hide from a user who can only administer some regions?
evals.expected_output: Via hook_form_FORM_ID_alter on block_admin_display_form and block_form it removes the block rows for regions the user cannot administer and strips those regions from the region-select dropdown options, so the user can neither see nor move blocks into disallowed regions.

evals.prompt: Does Block Region Permissions have a settings/configuration form, Drush commands, or module dependencies I need to install?
evals.expected_output: No. It has no configuration form, no settings, no config schema, and no Drush commands; it requires no modules beyond core's Block module. You configure it entirely on People > Permissions. Block Content Permissions is only a suggested companion, not a dependency.

evals.prompt: If I enable a new theme or a theme adds a new region, do I have to register new permissions in Block Region Permissions? How are the permissions produced?
evals.expected_output: No manual registration. Permissions are generated dynamically by Permissions::get() (declared via permission_callbacks in the .permissions.yml), which reads each enabled, non-hidden theme's regions from theme info; new themes/regions automatically produce new 'administer {theme} {region}' permissions.
